narrative.summary: You're on Main Street in Mount Pleasant, a few blocks from Published on Main, walking into high ceilings that pull your gaze up and a buzz that's packed but not loud—everyone leaning in over small plates and wine stems. The menu is small plates built around whatever's good right now, the crudo showing up in nearly every review for a reason. Whimsical without being precious, the kind of place where the architecture does enough work that they don't need to overdo the rest.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in Mount Pleasant, a few blocks from Published on Main, which makes sense once you step inside and see the same exacting eye for design. High ceilings pull your gaze up, and the room has that specific kind of buzz—packed but not loud, everyone leaning in over small plates and wine stems. It's whimsical without being precious, the kind of place where the architecture does enough work that they don't need to overdo the rest.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu is small plates built around whatever's good right now, the kind of cooking that makes local ingredients feel like discoveries instead of obligations. The crudo shows up in nearly every review for a reason—pristine fish, clean cuts, just enough citrus or oil to let it shine. Same with the steak, which people come back for two nights in a row. Desserts like strawberry tart and sorbet finish things off without weighing you down, and the whole meal pairs with whatever natural wine the staff steers you toward.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: This is your pre- or post-dinner stop if you're eating at Published on Main, but it's also worth the trip on its own when you want somewhere buzzy without the commitment of a full tasting menu. Come for an anniversary, or just because you want to try three wines you've never heard of and eat raw fish under high ceilings.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "My husband and I came here last night for the first time, for our wedding anniversary and we couldn’t have loved it more! The natural wine was some of the best we’ve had and all the food was amazing too. Highly recommend the steak and crudo. We will definitely be back!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "My friend and I loved this place so much, we visited two days in a row. The crudo and steak are amazing. Sorbet and strawberry tart are delicious. We found our favourite natural wine (De Sol a Sol) here thanks to the very knowledgeable server we had on Saturday evening. We came back to share dessert and wine on Sunday and were also given great service by Natasha. Great staff and amazing food/drink. We'll be back for sure."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The environment of this wine bar is beautiful with the tall ceilings. The servers were knowledgeable about the different type of wines but not that easy to understand. We were able to get a table for 6 as a walk in pretty easily.

The food servings were small but still meant for sharing. The price is justified if you are splitting between two or three people but a bit overpriced when just ordering it for yourself. For example a small loaf of bread and a table spoon of butter for $16.

They have a rotating list of wines by the glass but bottles ranging from $80 and up.

I am happy that I tried it and went but it definitely is just for the vibes rather than the food and prices."
